parts.text: prepare a new github release with a new binary and everything

The user wants to prepare a new GitHub release with a new binary and "everything". I need to understand what this project is and how releases are currently handled.

Let me first look at the project structure, check for existing release workflows/scripts, and understand the build process before asking clarifying questions or proceeding.

From AGENTS.md, I know this is "Keating" - a Pi-powered hyperteacher CLI tool + web app. It uses Bun, has a web component, and there's mention of a release workflow that bundles node_modules into a tarball.

Let me explore:
1. Current package.json version
2. Existing GitHub Actions/workflows for releases
3. Build scripts related to releases
4. What the binary is (bin/keating.js?)
5. Any release-related documentation

I should look at .github/workflows, package.json, scripts/, and any release-related files.
Let me first understand the current release setup and project state.

metadata.preview:
# Keating task runner — use `just <task>` to run any task
# All tasks run from the project root unless noted

# Default: list available tasks
default:
    @just --list

# Install dependencies (root + web)
install:
    bun install
    cd web && bun install

# Build the root TypeScript project
build:
    bun x tsc -p tsconfig.json
    bun scripts/copy-core-templates.ts

# Generate NodePod boot files from source tree
generate-nodepod-boot:
    bun scripts/generate-nodepod-boot-files.ts

state.title: justfile

parts.text: Let me look at the release workflow, package.json, and understand the versioning and build process before asking questions about what kind of release they want.

state.output:
<path>/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/.github/workflows/release.yml</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: name: Release
2: 
3: on:
4:   push:
5:     tags:
6:       - 'v*'
7: 
8: permissions:
9:   contents: write
10: 
11: jobs:
12:   build:
13:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
14:     strategy:
15:       matrix:
16:         include:
17:           - os: linux
18:             arch: x64
19:             target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
20:           - os: darwin
21:             arch: x64
22:             target: x86_64-apple-darwin
23:           - os: darwin
24:             arch: arm64
25:             target: aarch64-apple-darwin
26: 
27:     steps:
28:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
29: 
30:       - name: Setup Bun
31:         uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
32:         with:
33:           bun-version: latest
34: 
35:       - name: Build
36:         run: |
37:           bun install
38:           bun x tsc -p tsconfig.json
39:           bun scripts/copy-core-templates.ts
40: 
41:       - name: Create bundle
42:         run: |
43:           VERSION="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}"
44:           BUNDLE_NAME="keating-${VERSION}-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}"
45:           STAGE_DIR="${BUNDLE_NAME}"
46:           mkdir -p "${STAGE_DIR}"
47:           mkdir -p "${STAGE_DIR}/bin"
48:           cp bin/keating.js "${STAGE_DIR}/bin/"
49:           cp -r dist pi scripts/install "${STAGE_DIR}/"
50:           cp package.json "${STAGE_DIR}/"
51:           # Bundle node_modules so the tarball is truly standalone
52:           cp -r node_modules "${STAGE_DIR}/"
53:           mv "${STAGE_DIR}/install/install.sh" "${STAGE_DIR}/install.sh"
54:           rm -rf "${STAGE_DIR}/install"
55:           tar -czf "${BUNDLE_NAME}.tar.gz" "${STAGE_DIR}"
56: 
57:       - name: Verify bundle contents
58:         run: |
59:           VERSION="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}"
60:           BUNDLE_NAME="keating-${VERSION}-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}"
61:           tar -tzf "${BUNDLE_NAME}.tar.gz" > bundle-files.txt
62:           grep -Fx "${BUNDLE_NAME}/bin/keating.js" bundle-files.txt
63:           grep -Fx "${BUNDLE_NAME}/dist/src/cli/main.js" bundle-files.txt
64:           grep -Fx "${BUNDLE_NAME}/dist/src/pi/hyperteacher-extension.js" bundle-files.txt
65:           ! grep -F "${BUNDLE_NAME}/bin/.keating/" bundle-files.txt
66:           ! grep -F "${BUNDLE_NAME}/bin/keating.config.json" bundle-files.txt
67: 
68:       - name: Upload artifact
69:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
70:         with:
71:           name: keating-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
72:           path: keating-*.tar.gz
73: 
74:   release:
75:     needs: build
76:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
77:     steps:
78:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
79: 
80:       - name: Download artifacts
81:         uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
82:         with:
83:           path: artifacts
84: 
85:       - name: Create release
86:         uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
87:         with:
88:           files: artifacts/**/*.tar.gz
89:           generate_release_notes: true
90: 
91:   publish-npm:
92:     needs: release
93:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
94:     steps:
95:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
96: 
97:       - name: Setup Bun
98:         uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
99:         with:
100:           bun-version: latest
101: 
102:       - name: Build
103:         run: |
104:           bun install
105:           cd web
106:           bun install
107:           cd ..
108:           bun x tsc -p tsconfig.json
109:           bun scripts/copy-core-templates.ts
110: 
111:       - name: Publish to npm
112:         run: |
113:           npm pack --ignore-scripts --dry-run --json > npm-pack.json
114:           node scripts/verify-package-manifest.mjs npm-pack.json
115:           echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}" > ~/.npmrc
116:           npm publish --access public
117:         env:
118:           NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
119: 
120:   deploy-web:
121:     needs: release
122:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
123:     steps:
124:       - name: Deploy to Railway
125:         run: |
126:           echo "Web deployment handled by Railway GitHub integration"
127:           echo "Connect the repo at https://railway.app to enable auto-deploy"

(End of file - total 127 lines)
</content>

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: linux
            arch: x64
            target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
          - os: darwin

state.title: package.json

state.output:
<path>/home/diogenes/Projects/keating/scripts/install/install.sh</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/bin/sh
2: 
3: set -eu
4: 
5: VERSION="${1:-latest}"
6: INSTALL_BIN_DIR="${KEATING_INSTALL_BIN_DIR:-$HOME/.local/bin}"
7: INSTALL_APP_DIR="${KEATING_INSTALL_APP_DIR:-$HOME/.local/sharekeating}"
8: SKIP_PATH_UPDATE="${KEATING_INSTALL_SKIP_PATH_UPDATE:-0}"
9: path_action="already"
10: path_profile=""
11: 
12: step() {
13:   printf '==> %s\n' "$1"
14: }
15: 
16: run_with_spinner() {
17:   label="$1"
18:   shift
19: 
20:   if [ ! -t 2 ]; then
21:     step "$label"
22:     "$@"
23:     return
24:   fi
25: 
26:   "$@" &
27:   pid=$!
28:   frame=0
29: 
30:   set +e
31:   while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; do
32:     case "$frame" in
33:       0) spinner='|' ;;
34:       1) spinner='/' ;;
35:       2) spinner='-' ;;
36:       *) spinner='\\' ;;
37:     esac
38:     printf '\r==> %s %s' "$label" "$spinner" >&2
39:     frame=$(( (frame + 1) % 4 ))
40:     sleep 0.1
41:   done
42:   wait "$pid"
43:   status=$?
44:   set -e
45: 
46:   printf '\r\033[2K' >&2
47:   if [ "$status" -ne 0 ]; then
48:     printf '==> %s failed\n' "$label" >&2
49:     return "$status"
50:   fi
51: 
52:   step "$label"
53: }
54: 
55: normalize_version() {
56:   case "$1" in
57:     "")
58:       printf 'latest\n'
59:       ;;
60:     latest | stable)
61:       printf 'latest\n'
62:       ;;
63:     v*)
64:       printf '%s\n' "${1#v}"
65:       ;;
66:     *)
67:       printf '%s\n' "$1"
68:       ;;
69:   esac
70: }
71: 
72: download_file() {
73:   url="$1"
74:   output="$2"
75: 
76:   if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
77:     if [ -t 2 ]; then
78:       curl -fL --progress-bar "$url" -o "$output"
79:     else
80:       curl -fsSL "$url" -o "$output"
81:     fi
82:     return
83:   fi
84: 
85:   if command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
86:     if [ -t 2 ]; then
87:       wget --show-progress -O "$output" "$url"
88:     else
89:       wget -q -O "$output" "$url"
90:     fi
91:     return
92:   fi
93: 
94:   echo "curl or wget is required to install Keating." >&2
95:   exit 1
96: }
97: 
98: download_text() {
99:   url="$1"
100: 
101:   if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
102:     curl -fsSL "$url"
103:     return
104:   fi
105: 
106:   if command -v wget >/dev/null 2>&1; then
107:     wget -q -O - "$url"
108:     return
109:   fi
110: 
111:   echo "curl or wget is required to install Keating." >&2
112:   exit 1
113: }
114: 
115: add_to_path() {
116:   path_action="already"
117:   path_profile=""
118: 
119:   case ":$PATH:" in
120:     *":$INSTALL_BIN_DIR:"*)
121:       return
122:       ;;
123:   esac
124: 
125:   if [ "$SKIP_PATH_UPDATE" = "1" ]; then
126:     path_action="skipped"
127:     return
128:   fi
129: 
130:   profile="${KEATING_INSTALL_SHELL_PROFILE:-$HOME/.profile}"
131:   if [ -z "${KEATING_INSTALL_SHELL_PROFILE:-}" ]; then
132:     case "${SHELL:-}" in
133:       */zsh)
134:         profile="$HOME/.zshrc"
135:         ;;
136:       */bash)
137:         profile="$HOME/.bashrc"
138:         ;;
139:     esac
140:   fi
141: 
142:   path_profile="$profile"
143:   path_line="export PATH=\"$INSTALL_BIN_DIR:\$PATH\""
144:   if [ -f "$profile" ] && grep -F "$path_line" "$profile" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
145:     path_action="configured"
146:     return
147:   fi
148: 
149:   {
150:     printf '\n# Added by Keating installer\n'
151:     printf '%s\n' "$path_line"
152:   } >>"$profile"
153:   path_action="added"
154: }
155: 
156: require_command() {
157:   if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
158:     echo "$1 is required to install Keating." >&2
159:     exit 1
160:   fi
161: }
162: 
163: warn_command_conflict() {
164:   expected_path="$INSTALL_BIN_DIR/keating"
165:   resolved_path="$(command -v keating 2>/dev/null || true)"
166: 
167:   if [ -z "$resolved_path" ]; then
168:     return
169:   fi
170: 
171:   if [ "$resolved_path" != "$expected_path" ]; then
172:     step "Warning: current shell resolves keating to $resolved_path"
173:     step "Run now: export PATH=\"$INSTALL_BIN_DIR:\$PATH\" && hash -r && keating"
174:     step "Or launch directly: $expected_path"
175: 
176:     case "$resolved_path" in
177:       *"/node_modules/keating/"* | *"/node_modules/.bin/keating")
178:         step "If that path is an old global npm install, remove it with: npm uninstall -g keating"
179:         ;;
180:     esac
181:   fi
182: }
183: 
184: resolve_release_metadata() {
185:   normalized_version="$(normalize_version "$VERSION")"
186: 
187:   if [ "$normalized_version" = "latest" ]; then
188:     release_page="$(download_text "https://github.com/Diogenesoftoronto/keating/releases/latest")"
189:     resolved_version="$(printf '%s\n' "$release_page" | sed -n 's@.*releases/tag/v\([0-9][^"<>[:space:]]*\).*@\1@p' | head -n 1)"
190: 
191:     if [ -z "$resolved_version" ]; then
192:       echo "Failed to resolve the latest Keating release version." >&2
193:       exit 1
194:     fi
195:   else
196:     resolved_version="$normalized_version"
197:   fi
198: 
199:   bundle_name="keating-${resolved_version}-${asset_target}"
200:   archive_name="${bundle_name}.${archive_extension}"
201:   download_url="${KEATING_INSTALL_BASE_URL:-https://github.com/Diogenesoftoronto/keating/releases/download/v${resolved_version}}/${archive_name}"
202: 
203:   printf '%s\n%s\n%s\n%s\n' "$resolved_version" "$bundle_name" "$archive_name" "$download_url"
204: }
205: 
206: case "$(uname -s)" in
207:   Darwin)
208:     os="darwin"
209:     ;;
210:   Linux)
211:     os="linux"
212:     ;;
213:   *)
214:     echo "install.sh supports macOS and Linux. Use install.ps1 on Windows." >&2
215:     exit 1
216:     ;;
217: esac
218: 
219: case "$(uname -m)" in
220:   x86_64 | amd64)
221:     arch="x64"
222:     ;;
223:   arm64 | aarch64)
224:     arch="arm64"
225:     ;;
226:   *)
227:     echo "Unsupported architecture: $(uname -m)" >&2
228:     exit 1
229:     ;;
230: esac
231: 
232: require_command mktemp
233: require_command tar
234: require_command node
235: 
236: asset_target="$os-$arch"
237: archive_extension="tar.gz"
238: release_metadata="$(resolve_release_metadata)"
239: resolved_version="$(printf '%s\n' "$release_metadata" | sed -n '1p')"
240: bundle_name="$(printf '%s\n' "$release_metadata" | sed -n '2p')"
241: archive_name="$(printf '%s\n' "$release_metadata" | sed -n '3p')"
242: download_url="$(printf '%s\n' "$release_metadata" | sed -n '4p')"
243: 
244: step "Installing Keating ${resolved_version} for ${asset_target}"
245: 
246: tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
247: cleanup() {
248:   rm -rf "$tmp_dir"
249: }
250: trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM
251: 
252: archive_path="$tmp_dir/$archive_name"
253: step "Downloading ${archive_name}"
254: if ! download_file "$download_url" "$archive_path"; then
255:   cat >&2 <<EOF
256: Failed to download ${archive_name} from:
257:   ${download_url}
258: 
259: The ${asset_target} bundle is missing from the GitHub release.
260: This usually means the release exists, but not all platform bundles were uploaded.
261: 
262: Workarounds:
263:   - try again after the release finishes publishing
264:   - install via pnpm instead: pnpm add -g keating
265:   - install via bun instead: bun add -g keating
266: EOF
267:   exit 1
268: fi
269: 
270: mkdir -p "$INSTALL_APP_DIR"
271: rm -rf "$INSTALL_APP_DIR/$bundle_name"
272: run_with_spinner "Extracting ${archive_name}" tar -xzf "$archive_path" -C "$INSTALL_APP_DIR"
273: 
274: mkdir -p "$INSTALL_BIN_DIR"
275: step "Linking keating into $INSTALL_BIN_DIR"
276: cat >"$INSTALL_BIN_DIR/keating" <<EOF
277: #!/bin/sh
278: set -eu
279: exec node "$INSTALL_APP_DIR/$bundle_name/bin/keating.js" "\$@"
280: EOF
281: chmod 0755 "$INSTALL_BIN_DIR/keating"
282: 
283: add_to_path
284: 
285: case "$path_action" in
286:   added)
287:     step "PATH updated for future shells in $path_profile"
288:     step "Run now: export PATH=\"$INSTALL_BIN_DIR:\$PATH\" && hash -r && keating"
289:     ;;
290:   configured)
291:     step "PATH is already configured for future shells in $path_profile"
292:     step "Run now: export PATH=\"$INSTALL_BIN_DIR:\$PATH\" && hash -r && keating"
293:     ;;
294:   skipped)
295:     step "PATH update skipped"
296:     step "Run now: export PATH=\"$INSTALL_BIN_DIR:\$PATH\" && hash -r && keating"
297:     ;;
298:   *)
299:     step "$INSTALL_BIN_DIR is already on PATH"
300:     step "Run: hash -r && keating"
301:     ;;
302: esac
303: 
304: warn_command_conflict
305: 
306: printf 'Keating %s installed successfully.\n' "$resolved_version"
307: printf 'Run "keating" to start the hyperteacher shell.\n'
308: printf 'Or visit https://keating.help to use in browser.\n'

(End of file - total 308 lines)
</content>

#!/bin/sh

set -eu

VERSION="${1:-latest}"
INSTALL_BIN_DIR="${KEATING_INSTALL_BIN_DIR:-$HOME/.local/bin}"
INSTALL_APP_DIR="${KEATING_INSTALL_APP_DIR:-$HOME/.local/sharekeating}"
SKIP_PATH_UPDATE="${KEATING_INSTALL_SKIP_PATH_UPDATE:-0}"
path_action="already"
path_profile=""

step() {
  printf '==> %s\n' "$1"
}

  shift

- Build platform bundles (linux-x64, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64)

Done. Here's what just happened:

**Release `v1.2.0` is now live on GitHub.** The tag push triggered the [Release workflow](https://github.com/Diogenesoftoronto/keating/actions/workflows/release.yml), which will:

1. **Build platform bundles** for linux-x64, darwin-x64, and darwin-arm64 (each includes `node_modules` for standalone installs)
2. **Create the GitHub release** with auto-generated release notes
3. **Publish to npm** (if `NPM_TOKEN` secret is configured)
4. **Deploy web** (via Railway GitHub integration, if connected)

The [`install.sh`](https://github.com/Diogenesoftoronto/keating/releases/download/v1.2.0/install.sh) script will resolve `v1.2.0` for new installs once the workflow finishes (~5–10 min).

Your 20 uncommitted web changes are back in the working tree, untouched.
